title: WYSIWYG document editor using Milkdown, with history and annotation support
purpose: Provides a rich text editing experience backed by Milkdown, supporting document history, annotations, and keyboard shortcuts while maintaining compatibility with legacy document editor contracts.
module_context: Exports a React component DocumentEditorWysiwyg that implements a WYSIWYG editor using Milkdown, manages document state and annotations, handles save/cancel operations, and integrates with history and collapsible sections features.

steps.content: Resolve the target document from context or props.
Initialize editor state including content, changes flag, and saving status.
Set up annotations state by migrating legacy inline markers on first load.
Configure history modal state for version history display.
Set up refs for flush and milkdown handle access.
Initialize editor view state to trigger toolbar re-renders.
Reset local state when the target document changes.
Handle content changes from the editor and update React state.
Update the editor view state with the newly created view.
Update the latest markdown reference with the new content.
Update the React state content with the new markdown.
Mark the document as having unsaved changes.
Clear any previous error messages.
Notify parent component of the content change.
Validate that a document is selected before attempting to persist.
Update the document in the store with the new content and timestamp.
IF the update fails, capture and store the error message.
Store pending annotations in a global window object for server-side persistence.
IF storing fails, silently ignore the error as it's not critical.
Update the annotations state with the new annotations.
Mark the document as having unsaved changes.
Stash the updated annotations for potential server-side persistence.
Validate that a document is selected before attempting to save.
Set the saving state to true and clear any previous errors.
Force milkdown to flush pending markdown changes.
Determine the final content using the latest markdown reference.
Persist the document content and timestamp to the store.
Update the React state with the saved content and mark changes as saved.
Notify parent component of the saved document.
IF the save fails, capture and store the error message.
Reset the saving state after completion.
Revert the editor UI to show the original document content.
Update React state to reflect the original content.
Reset the changes flag and clear any error messages.
Close the history modal by setting its open state to false.
Reset history modal state to initial values.
Open the history modal by setting its open state to true.
Listen for keyboard events to trigger save on Ctrl/Cmd+S.
Prevent default browser behavior for the save shortcut.
Call the save handler when the save shortcut is detected.
Do nothing for Escape key press.
